channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Artisans in Guatemala hand-stitch velvet cloaks for Holy Week processions
item.description: SAN MIGUEL ESCOBAR, Guatemala (AP) — Stitch by stitch, artisans have worked for months on the elaborate garments that will debut this week in Holy Week processions across Guatemala and beyond.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Divided loyalties? 62 Taiwanese military members found with Chinese residency permits
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — At least 62 active-duty Taiwanese military members have been found holding Chinese residency permits, the self-ruled island's Defense Ministry said, in the latest revelation of Chinese influence in the Taiwanese armed forces.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China’s leader Xi Jinping arrives in Cambodia to wrap up 3-nation Southeast Asia tour
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping has arrive in Cambodia for a two-day state visit that serves as an opportunity to further strengthen already robust relations.
item.dc:creator: Grant Peck And Sopheng Cheang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Carney says Trump key issue in Canada's election, while Conservative rival says country needs change
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Mark Carney said Wednesday the key question in Canada's election is who is best to deal with U.S. President Donald Trump while his Conservative rival argued that Carney doesn't represent change after a decade of Liberal Party rule.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli defense minister says troops will remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s defense minister said on Wednesday that troops will remain in so-called security zones in the Gaza Strip, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely, remarks that could further complicate talks with Hamas over a ceasefire and hostage release.
item.dc:creator: ISAAC SCHARF, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dog reunites with Israeli family after disappearing for 18 months in Gaza
item.description: BINYAMINA, Israel (AP) — Rachel Dancyg never thought she would see her dog again after it disappeared in the Hamas attack that sparked the ongoing war with Israel. Her ex-husband and brother were abducted from Kibbutz Nir Oz in southern Israel and killed.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man gets life in prison in killing of transgender model in the South Caucasus nation of Georgia
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — A man was convicted Wednesday of stabbing to death a transgender actor and model in her apartment in Georgia last year, a killing that shocked the South Caucasus nation and came a day after lawmakers passed legislation that severely restricted LGBTQ+ rights.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands of Jews gather for traditional blessing in Jerusalem
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Tens of thousands of people gathered at the holiest Jewish prayer site in Jerusalem on Tuesday for the traditional priestly blessing. The “Birkat Cohanim,” or Cohen’s blessing, is a ritual dating back over 2,500 years to when King Solomon’s Temple stood on the same site.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Shootout rescues Tennessee pastor abducted in South Africa
item.description: Josh Sullivan, a self-described “church-planting missionary,” has been reunited with his wife and their two children.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrat asks government watchdog to audit Trump foreign assistance cuts
item.description: Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.), ranking member of the Senate Budget Committee and senior member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, is asking an independent government watchdog to analyze the consequences of President Trump’s foreign aid cuts.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Xi says China will stand with Southeast Asian countries in face of economic shocks
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping has told Malaysia’s leader that China will be a collaborative partner and stand with its Southeast Asian neighbors in the wake of global economic shocks.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran confirms that the 2nd round of nuclear talks with the US will be in Rome
item.description: Iranian state television has confirmed that the second round of Iran-U.S. nuclear talks will be held in Rome after earlier confusion over where the negotiations would take place.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell And Nasser Karimi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Tuesday convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The first lawsuit is filed over the deadly nightclub roof collapse in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The first known lawsuit has been filed against the owners of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed and killed 231 people in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Foreign Affairs Dems press Rubio on Burma earthquake response, USAID cuts
item.description: Rep. Gregory Meeks (D-N.Y.) and 18 other Democrats on the House Foreign Affairs Committee pressed the Trump administration to urgently provide aid for those impacted by the deadly Burma earthquake last month.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Hungary passes constitutional amendment to ban LGBTQ+ public events, seen as a major blow to rights
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungary’s parliament on Monday passed an amendment to the constitution that allows the government to ban public events by LGBTQ+ communities, a decision that legal scholars and critics call another step toward authoritarianism by the populist government.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: El Salvador's Bukele says he won’t return Abrego Garcia to US
item.description: President Nayib Bukele called Abrego Garcia “a terrorist” and said he had no power to return Abrego Garcia to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Poland says Moscow is 'mocking' Trump with deadly Ukraine strike
item.description: Poland is urging the U.S. to respond to Russian President Vladimir Putin's "mocking" response to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure peace deal by striking civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ecuador re-elects President Daniel Noboa amid nationwide unease over crime
item.description: Conservative Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa, a wealthy anti-crime crusader, was re-elected on Sunday, defeating left-wing lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fears of another narco-state rise as Latin American country readies for pivotal vote
item.description: Ecuador is headed to a second round of a presidential election in which incumbent President Daniel Noboa faces leftist challenger Luisa González as the nation faces gang violence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How a penguin in a cardboard box caused a helicopter crash in South Africa
item.description: A penguin inside a carboard box led to a helicopter crash in South Africa, the country's Civil Aviation Authority said in a report this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delegates from Iran, US holding talks in Oman amid ongoing tensions: What to know
item.description: Delegates from the United States and Iran are holding talks in Oman Saturday in a delicate effort to restart negotiations over Tehran’s controversial nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tariffs on China are 145%, White House clarifies, as stocks slump
item.description: World markets skyrocketed Thursday in the wake of President Donald Trump's 90-day retaliatory tariff pause.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
